ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Continued To Nov-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Franchesca Callejo, Esq., for Macina Taylor, Successor Administrator.
Nancy Lofdahl, Esq., for Denise Smith, Administrator of related Estate.

The following related matters are called:
PES-18-301880; PES-20-304064; PES-21-304527; and PES-21-304896.

At the court's query, counsel offers an update on the subject matters and the status of settlement. Ms. Lofdahl reports that there is a delay in listing and selling the Estate's real property due to a pending Unlawful Detainer matter and provides the court with the case number (CUD-24-675768). The court instructs counsel to file a petition to approve status of the estate in this matter and each related matter. Upon agreement by counsel and the court, the above-referenced petitions are to be filed by Nov-13-2024.

The subject matter is continued to Nov-27-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Lopez. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-21-2020 Continued To Nov-02-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Essie Carroll appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Francesca Callejo appeared by zoom.
Macina Taylor (Daughter of Ms. Carroll) by zoom.

The Court stated that it looks like a corrected inventory and appraisal needs to be filed and that the one that Ms. Carroll did not have the correct date of death. Ms. Callejo stated that she was contacted by Ms. Carroll shortly after the last hearing, and that she needs the opportunity to review the file to determine if she is going to take the case and do a substitution of Attorney. Ms. Callejo also stated that it doesn't seem to be too complicated, there are multiple owners of this one property, that one of the owners has passed away and that there seems to be a large medical bill. Status on close of estate is continued to November 2, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jun-22-2020 Continued To Aug-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Essie Carroll in Propria Persona.
Messina Taylor (Essie's Daughter) also present.

Ms. Taylor stated that they sent the inventory over to the Probate Referee (Ms. Simon) on Friday, that Ms. Simon will be reviewing it very soon, that the property was caught on fire last year in February and that the property is currently under construction. Ms. Taylor also stated that the construction should be done within three to six months and that the property was left to four siblings by the Grandfather (Estate of Artis Byrd PES-88-248938). The Court suggested getting a Lawyer to assist with the process. Status on close of estate is continued to August 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Continued To Mar-30-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Essie Carroll in Propria Persona.
Macina Taylor (Daughter of Essie Carroll) also present.
Janet Boessenecker for San Francisco Public Administrator.

Ms. Carroll stated that she was not at the last hearing because she didn't get notice. Ms. Taylor stated that Ms. Carroll now lives with her, that the house burned down last February, that she did not know anything about an inventory and appraisal, and verified her address. The Court informed Ms. Carroll that the inventory and appraisal, and either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have to be filed before May 23, 2020. Ms. Boessenecker noted that there is a possibility that the Probate Referee will not be able to get the inventory back by then. Status on close of estate is continued to March 30, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Singh. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator and the Attorney to appear on February 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the inventory and appraisal, and a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration have not been filed and to show cause as to why the Administrator should not be suspended. ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Endy Ukoha-Ajike appeared by telephone for Essie Carroll (Administrator).

Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that he took over this case from Attorney William Taylor in May of 2018, that the property burned down and has not been repaired yet, that when they sent out notices they received a $100,000.00 claim and that they are still sorting through that claim. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike also stated that they found out that title of the property is not only in the Decedent's name, that the Client is not cooperating, that he is on the verge of withdrawing from the case and that if the Client does not sign the consent to withdraw, he will file a motion to withdraw as Counsel. The Court stated that a status report explaining those facts would have been preferred. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that the Client won't give him filing fees and that his office is reluctant to pay fees that won't be reimbursed. The Court reminded Mr. Ukoha-Ajike of his duty to the Court and stated that this is not a very good excuse. Mr. Ukoha-Ajike stated that he will file a status report. Status on close of estate is continued to January 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: William Taylor for and with Essie Carroll (Petitioner).

The Court inquired as to what efforts were made in locating the heir. Mr. Taylor stated that they have access in his office to a program called Accurint, that the heir is the Daughter of the Decedent, that she was last known to be in Las Vegas and that they got some leads, but nothing turned out. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $10,000.00 is required. The Court will set a future date for status on close of estate and appoint a Probate Referee.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
